seo.title: Pydantic AI Guardrails for Education
seo.description: Compliance guide for Pydantic AI agents in education. Meet FERPA, COPPA requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Education teams using Pydantic AI for tutoring, grading, and personalized learning face heightened risks due to strict regulations like FERPA and COPPA governing student data privacy. Without robust compliance guardrails, AI agents can inadvertently expose sensitive student information, generate biased or inappropriate content, or fail accessibility standards, leading to severe legal and reputational consequences. Vex provides essential observability and validation to ensure Pydantic AI deployments remain compliant and trustworthy in educational environments.

complianceRequirements.description: Protects student education records from unauthorized disclosure, requiring parental consent for minors and strict access controls.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex result validators block PII leakage in AI outputs and log access patterns for audit trails, preventing FERPA violations.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates verifiable parental consent before collecting personal information from children under 13.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex detects and redacts child-identifiable data in agent responses, ensuring compliance during interactions with minors.

complianceRequirements.description: Vary by state but generally require data minimization, encryption, and breach notification for student records.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex enforces data minimization policies and provides tamper-proof compliance logs for state audits.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires educational technology to be accessible to users with disabilities, including screen reader compatibility.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex validates structured outputs for WCAG compliance and flags inaccessible content generation.

industryRisks.name: Student PII Exposure
industryRisks.description: AI tutor accidentally includes student names, IDs, or grades in shared responses or logs.
industryRisks.example: Grading agent outputs 'Johnny Doe (ID: 12345) scored 78% on algebra quiz' to parent portal.

industryRisks.name: Inappropriate Content Generation
industryRisks.description: AI generates age-inappropriate or biased educational materials for K-12 students.
industryRisks.example: History tutor references mature themes unsuitable for elementary students.

industryRisks.name: Lack of Parental Consent Tracking
industryRisks.description: AI processes minor student data without verified parental consent documentation.
industryRisks.example: Personalized learning agent adapts content for 10-year-old without consent verification.

industryRisks.name: Accessibility Failures
industryRisks.description: Structured AI outputs fail screen reader compatibility for disabled students.
industryRisks.example: Math tutor generates complex LaTeX without accessible alt-text equivalents.

implementation.title: FERPA PII Redaction Validator
implementation.description: Add Vex result validator to block student PII in Pydantic AI tutor outputs.

implementation.title: Age-Appropriate Content Guardrail
implementation.description: Validate all educational content meets age-appropriate standards using Vex policies.
implementation.code:
from pydantic_ai import Tool
from vex_sdk import Vex

vex = Vex(api_key="your-vex-key")

@Tool()
def generate_lesson(grade_level: int, topic: str) -> str:
    """Generate age-appropriate lesson content"""
    content = f"Lesson for grade {grade_level} on {topic}"
    # Vex validates content appropriateness
    policy_result = vex.policy("education-age-appropriate").validate(content)
    if not policy_result.valid:
        raise ValueError(f"Content blocked: {policy_result.reason}")
    return content

# Integrates automatically with Pydantic AI agent definitions

implementation.title: Accessibility Output Validator
implementation.description: Ensure structured responses meet Section 508 standards before delivery to students.
implementation.code:
from pydantic_ai import Agent
from vex_sdk import Vex, Policy

class AccessibleMathTutor(Agent):
    def solve_equation(self, equation: str) -> dict:
        solution = self._call_llm(f"Solve: {equation}")
        structured = self.parse_to_model(solution)
        
        # Vex Section 508 validation
        accessibility_policy = Policy(
            name="section-508-math-output",
            rules=[
                "contains.alt_text",
                "mathml.accessible",
                "no.complex.tables"
            ]
        )
        
        vex.validate(structured, accessibility_policy)
        return structured
    
# Usage: tutor.solve_equation("2x + 3 = 7")

caseStudyScenario.heading: Pydantic AI in Education: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
Maplewood School District deployed Pydantic AI tutors across 15 elementary schools to provide personalized math instruction for 3rd-5th graders. The AI agents used structured Pydantic models to generate practice problems, provide step-by-step explanations, and track individual student progress. Teachers integrated the system into daily math blocks, with student performance data feeding back into the models for adaptive difficulty adjustment.

However, during the pilot, several compliance issues emerged. The AI began referencing individual student names and scores in generated problem sets visible to the entire class, violating FERPA. Content occasionally included advanced concepts inappropriate for 8-year-olds, and some math explanations used complex notation inaccessible to students using screen readers. Without proper guardrails, the district faced potential parental lawsuits and loss of federal funding.
caseStudyScenario.outcome:
After integrating Vex result validators on all Pydantic AI agent definitions, Maplewood achieved 100% compliance. Vex's FERPA policy automatically redacted PII from 847 outputs during the first month, while age-appropriateness validation blocked 23 instances of advanced content. Accessibility checks ensured all math explanations included proper MathML and alt-text, passing district WCAG audits.

The district maintained accreditation, avoided $2.3M in potential FERPA fines, and scaled the program district-wide with full parental consent tracking via Vex audit logs.

cta.heading: Comply with Education Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Install Vex today with `pip install vex-sdk` and add result validators to your Pydantic AI agents for instant education compliance.
